tasks.description: Preserve the existing shot-group click-selection path while touching timeline-agent plumbing: keep `TimelineCanvas` label clicks calling `onSelectClips(group.clipIds)`, keep `TimelineEditor` wiring that prop to `selectClips`, and do not introduce a redundant shot-group selection callback or second selection path.

Extend the shared selected-clip contract with optional shot metadata on both sides of the boundary. Update frontend persisted attachment types in `src/tools/video-editor/types/agent-session.ts` and backend payload types in `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/types.ts` so `shotId`/`shotName` and `shot_id`/`shot_name` are accepted without breaking legacy sessions, gallery attachments, or older calls.

Plumb shot metadata through the frontend send path. Update `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useSelectedMediaClips.ts` to resolve shot membership from `useTimelineEditorData().data?.config.pinnedShotGroups` plus `useShots()` data, preserve `shotId`/`shotName` when `AgentChat` merges timeline and gallery selections, and include `shot_id`/`shot_name` in `useSendMessage()` request bodies. Do not widen `ResolvedTimelineConfig` just to carry pinned groups unless execution uncovers a separate hard need.

Preserve shot metadata through backend normalization, persistence, and continuation recovery. Update `normalizeSelectedClips()`, `attachSelectedClips()`, and `recoverSelectedClipsFromTurns()` so explicit shot fields survive request parsing, persisted turn attachments, and follow-up turns. Keep prompt enrichment behavior intact and never overwrite explicit shot metadata supplied by the frontend.

Add explicit shot-name loading and structured timeline summary output on the backend. Extend `loadTimelineState()` to batch-load shot names for pinned shot IDs from `shots`, thread `shotNamesById` through `TimelineState`, `ToolContext`, and the `view_timeline` path, and update timeline formatting so clip lines include shot context and the summary includes a `Shot groups:` section with resolved `shotName` or `shotId`, `shotId`, `trackId`, `clipIds`, and `mode`.

Centralize same-shot semantics in one backend helper and apply it everywhere that depends on shot context. Add a shared resolver in `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/tools/clips.ts` that prefers explicit `shot_id`, then pinned-shot-group membership for timeline clips, then `findShotForGenerations()` as fallback; use it from `runAgentLoop()`, `executeCreateTask()`, `executeSetLora()`, and `executeDuplicateGeneration()`. Keep `duplicate_generation` on the fast path when selected clips already share a `shot_id`, and update prompt/system-prompt text so shared-shot context is surfaced once structured shot data is available.

Update existing automated suites only; do not add new test files. Extend current edge and frontend tests to cover: shot metadata normalization and recovery, selected-clip prompt shot context, timeline summary shot-group output, same-shot helper behavior and duplicate fast path, frontend attachment preservation/send payloads, and the existing `TimelineCanvas` shot-group single-click selection regression. Prefer adding cases to existing suites such as `selectedClips.test.ts`, `loop.test.ts`, `tools/clips.test.ts`, `tools/create-task.test.ts`, `tools/timeline.test.ts`, `AgentChat.test.tsx`, `useAgentSession.test.tsx`, and `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx`.

Run verification to completion and fix any failures before re-running. Execute focused edge tests with `npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.edge.config.ts supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/selectedClips.test.ts supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/loop.test.ts supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/tools/clips.test.ts supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/tools/create-task.test.ts supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/tools/timeline.test.ts`, execute focused frontend tests with `npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAgentSession.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx`, and, before finishing, write a short throwaway script that reproduces the shot-context bug end to end, run it to confirm the fix, then delete the script.

watch_items: Use `useTimelineEditorData().data?.config.pinnedShotGroups` for frontend shot membership resolution; `ResolvedTimelineConfig` does not expose `pinnedShotGroups`.
Keep the existing `TimelineCanvas` -> `onSelectClips(group.clipIds)` -> `TimelineEditor.selectClips` path unchanged; that behavior already exists and already has regression coverage.
Shot metadata must stay optional end to end so older sessions, stored turns, and gallery-only attachments remain valid.
Gallery selections may legitimately have `generationId` without any shot metadata; do not invent fake shot names on the frontend.
Do not overwrite explicit `shot_id` or `shot_name` from the client when enriching clips from generation metadata.
Backend timeline summaries cannot derive shot names from `pinnedShotGroups` alone; batch-load names from `shots` and fall back to raw `shotId` when needed.
One shared resolver should define same-shot semantics for loop defaults, active reference lookup, task shot reuse, travel LoRAs, and duplicate behavior.
`executeDuplicateGeneration()` should skip `findShotForGenerations()` when selected clips already provide a single shared explicit `shot_id`.
Do not broaden this work into unrelated AgentChat rendering or undo/realtime behavior changes; existing debt in those areas should not get worse.
Do not add new test files; extend existing suites and keep new or modified files reasonably small.
If runtime `run(command="view")` and loop-time summaries diverge, treat that as a bug; both paths must use the same shot-name data.
Manual smoke is useful after tests: select a pinned shot group, send an agent message, continue the session, and confirm shot context survives the continuation turn.

sense_checks.question: Did the timeline UI keep the existing shot-group single-click selection contract intact, with no redundant `onShotGroupSelect`-style path added?
Do frontend attachments and backend selected-clip payloads both accept optional shot id/name fields without breaking legacy call sites or stored data?
Does the frontend resolve shot metadata from `data?.config.pinnedShotGroups`, preserve it through `AgentChat`, and send `shot_id` and `shot_name` in `selected_clips` for timeline-selected media?
Do normalization, persisted attachments, and continuation recovery round-trip shot metadata without clobbering explicit client-supplied values?
Does timeline state now load shot names from `shots`, and does the timeline summary include both clip-level shot annotations and a `Shot groups:` section with resolved names or `shotId` fallback?
Do `runAgentLoop`, `executeCreateTask`, `executeSetLora`, and `executeDuplicateGeneration` all rely on one shared shot resolver that prefers explicit shot metadata before fallback inference?

Were the required coverage gaps closed by extending existing test suites only, including timeline summaries, prompt/roundtrip behavior, duplicate fast path, send payloads, and the existing shot-group click-selection regression?
Did the targeted Vitest runs pass after any needed fixes, and was the temporary reproduction script run successfully and then removed?

meta_commentary: Treat this as one shared shot-context pipeline, not a set of isolated patches. The only accepted correction to the approved plan is the frontend source-path fix already called out by gate review: `useSelectedMediaClips()` must read pinned groups from editor `data.config`, not `resolvedConfig`. Keep the existing shot-group click UX untouched, avoid unrelated refactors in AgentChat or timeline persistence, and make the backend shot resolver the single source of truth so summaries, prompts, travel defaults, LoRA updates, and duplicate semantics cannot drift apart.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Preserve the existing shot-group selection path instead of adding new canvas selection plumbing.
Extend shared selected-clip and attachment types with optional shot metadata.
Enrich frontend-selected clips with shot context and send that metadata to the agent.
Preserve shot metadata through backend normalization, attachment persistence, and continuation recovery.
Load explicit shot names on the backend and include structured shot context in timeline summaries.
Centralize same-shot resolution across loop, task creation, LoRA updates, duplicate behavior, and prompt guidance.
Add focused backend and frontend coverage for shot summaries, roundtrip metadata, prompt context, duplicate fast path, and send-path behavior.

validation.completeness_notes: All approved plan steps are mapped. Execution guidance incorporates the gate-approved correction for step 3 by sourcing pinned groups from `useTimelineEditorData().data?.config.pinnedShotGroups`. Step 7 is satisfied by extending existing test suites rather than adding new test files, because this finalize brief must honor the explicit execution constraint against creating new test files.
